language.label: Language

sources.title: Sources
source.type.help: Choose how DocGraph should read this documentation source.
source.name: Name
source.dsn.help.local: Local Markdown directory path, file:// path, Windows path, or WSL path.
source.dsn.help.static: Local mixed static docs directory. Supports Markdown, HTML, and TXT.
source.dsn.help.html: Local directory containing generated HTML files.
source.dsn.help.sftp: Remote directory DSN, for example sftp://user@example.com:22/var/www/docs.
source.dsn.help.git: Existing local Git checkout path. Remote URLs require an explicit remote clone directory.
source.dsn.help.confluence: Confluence base URL. Page ID is required before sync.
source.dsn.help.webdocs: Online docs host or path, for example https://docs.example.com or docs.example.com/guide.
source.max_pages.help: Blank means unlimited. Set a cap only when you want to control sync size.
source.include_children.help: Also sync all descendant pages of the specified page.
source.headers_json.help: Extra request headers sent while crawling.
source.is_spa.label: Enable JS rendering
source.is_spa.help: Enable for sites that require JavaScript to render content (e.g. Vue, React apps). Auto-detected when possible.
source.sync_in_progress: A sync task is already running for this source.
source.product.help: Optional label that creates a Product node. All documents from this source will be linked to this product in the knowledge graph.
source.module.help: Optional label that creates a Module node (a sub-component of the product). Documents are linked to this module, and the module is linked to the product with a "contains" edge.
search.title: Search
node.title: Node Explorer
node.help.title: How nodes and edges are built from documents
node.help.content:
When you sync a documentation source, DocGraph automatically builds a knowledge graph:

Nodes — entities extracted from your docs:
Product — created from the optional "Product" field on a source. Groups related docs together.
Module — created from the optional "Module" field. Represents a sub-component of a product.
Document — one node per document (Markdown file, HTML page, etc.).
DocSection — one node per heading/section within a document.
API — extracted from HTTP method + path patterns in the text (e.g. GET /api/users).
Edges — relationships between nodes:
contains — nesting (Product→Module, Document→DocSection).
describes — a product/module describes a document; or a section describes an API (OpenAPI docs).
mentions — a DocSection mentions an API.
exposes_api — a document/product/module exposes an API.
links_to — HTML <a> links between sections.

Node IDs are deterministic — resyncing the same source produces the same graph.
node.search.placeholder: document title, API, module, node id
